local_paths_new_worktree_would_not_have: any local Docker/PostgreSQL runtime state under ignored evidence paths

shared_external_services.docker: Not executed. Devcontainer file exists; no compose file found in inspected repo scope.
shared_external_services.postgresql_local: Not contacted. Local PostgreSQL-oriented code exists and ignored evidence/local_pg state exists.
shared_external_services.supabase: Not contacted. App and loaders use DB_URL_* variables when explicitly authorized.
shared_external_services.streamlit: Not started. Runtime entry is streamlit_app.py/app/Home.py; devcontainer auto-starts Streamlit when used.

topology_options.ease: HIGH when created from UI, MEDIUM for local parity with Claude
topology_options.windows_compatibility: HIGH if setup avoids symlinks and relies on PowerShell
topology_options.disk_cost: MEDIUM per worktree plus venv/data decisions
topology_options.setup: Best with versioned safe setup script and no automatic DB/Docker/data copy
topology_options.branching: Usually detached HEAD initially; create branch only when writing
topology_options.handoff: Good for committed/staged Git state, weak for ignored files
topology_options.ignored_data: Not transferred; must use external references or tracked fixtures
topology_options.secrets: Must not inherit or copy by default
topology_options.docker: Shared daemon risk if used by multiple worktrees
topology_options.debugging: Good for code/test isolation; less good for local service state
topology_options.recovery: Good if commits exist; untracked/ignored files need explicit checklist
topology_options.risk_of_loss: LOW with commits, MEDIUM with untracked changes
topology_options.claude_compatibility: Conceptually compatible but not identical to Claude local skills
topology_options.assessment: Good default for isolated implementation and independent review.

topology_options.summary: One or more long-lived Codex worktrees.
topology_options.setup: Can amortize setup cost but accumulates hidden state
topology_options.branching: Must avoid checking out the same branch in two worktrees
topology_options.docker: Tempting but risky if mixed with unrelated work
topology_options.assessment: Premature except possibly one LOCAL_LAB after a pilot proves value.

topology_options.windows_compatibility: HIGH for normal worktrees; symlinks/junctions remain conditional
topology_options.claude_compatibility: HIGH as a plain filesystem checkout
topology_options.assessment: Good fallback when Codex-managed UI behavior is insufficient or unavailable.

topology_options.handoff: Simple only while one phase is active
topology_options.ignored_data: All local state available but not reproducible
topology_options.recovery: Weak when many untracked items accumulate
topology_options.assessment: Keep only as LOCAL_OPERATIONAL, not as the default for concurrent agent work.

topology_options.summary: Main checkout for local operations, ephemeral worktrees for implementation/review, one deliberate lab when authorized.
topology_options.isolation: HIGH where needed, LOW only for Bastian-local operations
topology_options.setup: Safe setup for code worktrees; lab setup separate and explicit
topology_options.branching: One branch per write worktree; detached HEAD for pure validation
topology_options.handoff: Use commits/branches/PRs for code, checklist for ignored dependencies
topology_options.ignored_data: External references, tracked fixtures, or worktree-local temp only
topology_options.recovery: Strong if cleanup checklist is followed
topology_options.cleanup: Ephemeral by default; permanent only after evidence
topology_options.claude_compatibility: HIGH conceptually; Claude can use manual worktree or main checkout

selected_topology.decision: Use the main checkout only as LOCAL_OPERATIONAL and evidence hub; use short-lived worktrees for CODEX_IMPLEMENTATION and INDEPENDENT_REVIEW; create one LOCAL_LAB worktree only after explicit authorization; keep PLATFORM_TOOLING in ephemeral worktrees until recurrence proves a permanent lane is worth it.
why: It stops dirty-main from being the normal place for unrelated agent changes.
It recognizes that ignored local files, data, secrets, and lab state do not move with Git handoff.
It supports larger work by scaling isolation to risk instead of creating one worktree per tiny task.
It avoids prematurely maintaining permanent worktrees.

lane_design.purpose: Bastian manual operations, real Excel files, local secrets, productive commands when explicitly authorized, and final inspection.
allowed_baselines: main local with known dirty state
credentials: DB_URL_APP only for app/runtime when Bastian authorizes
DB_URL_LOAD only for explicitly authorized productive loader phases
DB_URL_CODEX_RO only for read-only checks when explicitly authorized
lane_design.data: May reference real local data read-only or productively only under explicit phase authorization.
lane_design.notes: This lane must not be treated as clean just because future worktrees exist.

lane_design.host: ephemeral Codex-managed or manual git worktree
lane_design.purpose: Scoped code or research-artifact implementation with exact staged set.
allowed_baselines: origin/main known local after user-approved sync
feature branch if the task is explicitly a continuation
lane_design.data: Tracked fixtures or synthetic temp files by default; real data only read-only with explicit decision.
lane_design.notes: Create a branch only when writing. Use detached HEAD for read-only validation.

lane_design.purpose: Adversarial validation of a commit without inheriting dirty-main or author-local artifacts.
lane_design.data: Synthetic fixtures or tracked files only unless task explicitly authorizes read-only data names/sizes.
lane_design.notes: Should not create branch unless review produces authorized report artifacts.

lane_design.host: one permanent or semi-permanent manual worktree, not created yet
lane_design.purpose: Docker/PostgreSQL clean-room rebuilds and local-only parity laboratories.
allowed_baselines: feature branch lab/<phase_id>-<slug> when lab scripts are being changed
credentials: DB_URL_CODEX_RO only for authorized read-only extracts
never DB_URL_LOAD or DB_URL_APP by default
lane_design.data: External reference to data/evidence roots or generated lab outputs under ignored worktree-local paths.
lane_design.notes: Prefer a single lab owner/phase at a time to avoid shared Docker/PostgreSQL collisions.

lane_design.purpose: Versioned safe setup scripts, skills, validation helpers, and Local Environment action definitions without product logic changes.
lane_design.data: No real data. Synthetic fixtures only.
lane_design.notes: Do not make this permanent until two or more real platform phases reuse it.

baseline_policy.new_implementation: Start from origin/main known local only after checking status and whether origin/main is ancestor of HEAD. If network sync is needed, ask Bastian before fetch/pull/rebase.
baseline_policy.independent_validation: Start from exact commit SHA under review, preferably detached HEAD, with baseline_commit recorded in the report.
baseline_policy.historical_reproduction: Start from exact historical commit. Do not rebase; preserve the commit being reproduced.
baseline_policy.db_lab: Start from explicit lab contract commit. Do not infer local_pg state is reproducible unless rebuild recipe and manifests prove it.
baseline_policy.local_changes_allowed: Only LOCAL_OPERATIONAL may intentionally start from dirty local state, and the inherited dirty files must be listed before work.
baseline_policy.local_changes_prohibited: CODEX_IMPLEMENTATION and INDEPENDENT_REVIEW must not begin from dirty-main as their implicit baseline.
baseline_policy.origin_advanced_detection: Use local refs first; if push is rejected as non-fast-forward, stop and ask Bastian. Do not auto-fetch/rebase in guarded phases.
baseline_policy.agent_remote_publish: If another agent publishes while work is in progress, stop integration, record local branch HEAD, compare only after explicit sync authorization.
baseline_policy.rebase_policy: Rebase only after Bastian authorizes integration. Never rebase independent validation or historical reproduction work.

branch_policy.phase_id: Use the exact phase id from the prompt, shortened only for filesystem safety.
branch_policy.slug: Short noun phrase for the target, lowercase ASCII, hyphen-separated.
branch_policy.date: Add date only for long-running lab/permanent lanes where retention requires it.
branch_policy.detached_head: Use for read-only reviews and historical reproduction.
branch_policy.create_branch_here: Use when the task authorizes file writes or commits from a worktree.
branch_policy.handoff: Use for committed or intentionally staged Git state plus checklist; never rely on it for ignored data/secrets.
branch_policy.pull_request: Use for multi-file implementation, risky runtime changes, or cross-agent review. Direct main push can continue only for explicitly scoped research artifacts or small validated changes when prompt authorizes it.
branch_policy.github_direct_commit_conflict: If GitHub/main advances, do not force push; stop and provide Bastian merge/rebase choices.

.venv.rule: Never copy blindly. Recreate per strategy or use external shared cache.

.env.rule: Never copy, read, stage, or transfer through handoff.

.env.*.rule: Only .env.example may be versioned. Other env files stay local.

.local_secrets.rule: Names/existence may be inspected; content never read unless a specific safe helper is authorized.

data.rule: Real workbooks stay in main/local data root. Worktrees use STOCK_ZERO_DATA_ROOT or explicit read-only path when authorized.

evidence.rule: Heavy historical evidence is not copied. New evidence must be phase-scoped and explicitly authorized.

caches.rule: __pycache__, pytest cache, Streamlit cache, and build caches may be deleted only inside disposable worktrees after status check.

outputs_temporales.rule: Use %TEMP% by default. Persistent outputs require explicit artifact path.

docker_volumes.rule: Never delete automatically. Name and owner must be documented by LOCAL_LAB phase.

local_config.rule: .streamlit/config.toml and editor settings may be local unless intentionally versioned.

generated.rule: Generated SQL/reports/evidence are classified by phase before staging.

python_environment_strategy.recommendation: Use a separate .venv per worktree, backed by shared pip/cache directories. Do not copy the main .venv.

comparison.PY_A_PER_WORKTREE_VENV: Most reproducible and isolated, but slower and duplicates packages.
comparison.PY_B_SHARED_VENV: Fast and small, but path collisions and dependency drift can cross-contaminate lanes.
comparison.PY_C_CENTRAL_CACHE_PER_WORKTREE_VENV: Best initial balance: isolated envs with shared download/build cache.
comparison.PY_D_SYSTEM_PYTHON: Lowest setup but weak reproducibility and high collision risk.

windows_rules: Detect Python 3.12 first for parity with current .venv.
Allow Python 3.11 only for devcontainer compatibility tasks after smoke.
Do not install dependencies automatically unless setup phase explicitly authorizes it.

options.STOCK_ZERO_DATA_ROOT: Preferred for authorized data access; value is local and not printed.
options.ignored_manifest: Useful later for local machine setup, but must not store secrets or customer rows.
options.partial_fixture_copy: Good for tests; only synthetic or sanitized tracked fixtures.
options.symlink_or_junction: Not initial default because Windows permissions and deletion semantics are risky.

never_background: long local-lab rebuilds without Bastian monitoring

principles: No secrets are copied into worktrees.
Lane-specific env injection beats inheritance from the main shell.

LOCAL_OPERATIONAL: DB_URL_LOAD when productive loader is explicitly authorized
LOCAL_LAB: local PostgreSQL DSN when lab phase is authorized
DB_URL_CODEX_RO only for explicit read-only extract

secret_strategy.verification: Use scripts/codex_ro_env_check.py or preflight helpers for DB-aware phases; otherwise use name-only/presence-only checks.

docker_strategy.selected: Single explicit LOCAL_LAB owner; no automatic Docker from setup.
rules: Docker daemon is shared across worktrees.
Use non-default ports only when parallel services are intentionally needed.
Volumes are never removed by automated cleanup.
docker compose down -v is forbidden unless Bastian authorizes the exact lab.
Only one agent may mutate the same local PostgreSQL lab at a time.
Clean-room reproducibility must be shown by rebuild scripts, manifests, hashes, and no reliance on hidden local_pg state.

local_environment.schema_note: No .codex folder exists in the repo, so 001B must verify the Local Environment schema through the Codex UI before committing any .codex configuration.
setup_script_concept: Print repo root, branch, HEAD, and Python candidate.
Refuse to run DB, Docker, loaders, refresh, or data copy.
Create or select per-worktree .venv only in authorized implementation phase.
Install requirements only when the setup phase explicitly allows dependency installation.
Run import smoke for streamlit, pandas, sqlalchemy, psycopg2, openpyxl, reportlab, plotly.
Run scanner or targeted smoke only, not full tests by default.
Fail with JSON-ish concise output and no secrets.

flows.worktree_to_local: Commit or exact diff plus checklist; ignored dependencies listed separately.
flows.local_to_worktree: Prefer commit/branch baseline; do not copy dirty-main files unless explicitly scoped.
flows.worktree_to_branch_pr: Push branch or open PR when change is multi-file/risky.
flows.commit_created: Record commit SHA, staged files, tests, preflight, and residual risk.
flows.changes_without_commit: Do not hand off as implicit state; provide patch/diff and untracked file list.

archive_thread_when: no uncommitted or untracked work remains in that worktree
preserve_worktree_when: lab state is expensive and documented
make_permanent_when: same lane is reused at least twice
hidden state is documented and intentionally retained
before_remove: confirm no ignored local artifacts are needed
cleanup_contract.safe_prune: Run git worktree prune only after listing worktrees and confirming no path contains needed uncommitted work.
cleanup_contract.retention: Ephemeral worktrees: remove after merge/review. Lab worktrees: explicit expiry or next lab decision.
never_auto_delete: untracked research/evidence not classified by Bastian

emergency.lost_worktree: Check git reflog and branch refs before deletion; search for commits by timestamp.
emergency.branch_locked: Use different branch or remove inactive worktree only after status check.
emergency.rebase_conflict: Abort only if user explicitly authorizes; otherwise preserve branch and ask.
emergency.handoff_incomplete: Stop integration and rebuild checklist from source worktree.
emergency.setup_failed: Do not repair globally; capture setup log and leave worktree intact.
emergency.obsolete_baseline: Record baseline and ask whether to rebase, merge, or restart.

legacy_dirty_checkout_plan.action: Do not modify. Bastian/owning phase must decide whether this tracked loader change is valid, superseded, or needs separate validation.

legacy_dirty_checkout_plan.status: untracked plus many ignored heavy artifacts
legacy_dirty_checkout_plan.action: Do not move/delete. Classify by phase, manifest, and sensitivity before any Git action.
Do not absorb automatically. Review against AI_RESEARCH_PROTOCOL before promotion.
Do not stage. Needs explicit SQL/design phase.
Do not stage. Needs explicit SQL/design phase.

recommended_pilot.why: It is scoped, currently blocked before first use, has clear tests, and can prove isolation, setup, targeted tests, commit, handoff, and cleanup without touching DB/Docker/data.

not_selected.CG005F_revalidation: Better for independent review but may need current dirty/evidence context.
not_selected.CG005G_local_lab: Too much Docker/PostgreSQL state for first platform pilot.
not_selected.small_documental_task: Too low risk to validate the worktree system fully.
not_selected.no_pilot_yet: Unnecessary because a good low-risk correction candidate exists.

baseline: collisions: count cross-phase file overlap incidents
files_outside_scope: count modified/staged paths not in prompt
test_reproducibility: same target test result across worktrees
metrics.progress_rule: Adopt next platform layer only when it reduces at least one cost/risk metric without increasing unbounded maintenance.

mandatory_critique.can_worktrees_add_complexity_without_sol: Yes. Worktrees isolate tracked checkout state but do not solve ignored data, secrets, Docker volumes, local DB state, or dirty-main debt.
mandatory_critique.still_depends_on_primary_checkout: Real Excel files, local secrets, Bastian operational commands, existing dirty research/evidence, and final human inspection.
mandatory_critique.ignored_files_make_reproducibility_misle: .env, .local_secrets, data, evidence payloads, .venv, Docker/local_pg state, caches, and generated outputs.
mandatory_critique.codex_design_not_shareable_with_claude: Codex-managed Local Environment UI schema and handoff mechanics may not map directly to Claude; shared rules must live in Git-visible docs/scripts, not private UI state.
mandatory_critique.automatic_local_environment_risks: Accidental installs, hidden DB/Docker starts, inherited credentials, expensive tests, data copy, and false confidence from green setup.
mandatory_critique.do_not_automate: Loader apply, product refresh, DB writes, Supabase changes, Docker volume deletion, force push, broad cleanup, data/secret copy.
mandatory_critique.permanent_worktrees_premature: Yes except a future LOCAL_LAB after a pilot; permanent lanes can accumulate hidden state.
mandatory_critique.lowest_sufficient_complexity: Hybrid with ephemeral implementation/review worktrees, main as operational lane, and one explicit lab only when needed.

implementation_phase_001b.goal: Implement the minimal safe worktree/Local Environment pilot without applying product changes.
files_that_may_be_created_after_approval: .codex/* only after UI schema is verified locally
ui_configuration_to_verify: How setup scripts are linked to Codex-managed worktrees
versionable_scripts: PowerShell setup/check scripts with no secrets and no DB/Docker execution
local_ignored_scripts: data root manifests if they contain personal paths or sensitive names
tests: targeted unit test in pilot worktree
implementation_phase_001b.first_worktree: PLATFORM_005B sz-load-observation correction worktree, branch codex/PLATFORM_005B-load-observation-correction, if Bastian approves.
rollback: Do not delete worktree until status is clean or committed.
Remove only created .codex/scripts files via exact paths.
Preserve branch and report if setup fails.
implementation_phase_001b.expected_commit: platform: add safe Codex worktree environment

bastian_decisions: Approve OPTION_E_HYBRID as the operating topology.
Approve whether direct push to main remains allowed for exact-scope research artifacts.
Approve PLATFORM_005B as first worktree pilot or choose another pilot.
Decide whether LOCAL_LAB should ever be permanent before CG005G.
Decide whether STOCK_ZERO_DATA_ROOT should become the standard local data pointer.
Confirm whether .codex Local Environment configuration may be versioned after UI schema verification.
commands_run: name-only and size-only local sensitive directory inventory
